definition: Only three FX factors survive Harvey-Liu-Zhu multiple-testing thresholds (t-stat ≥ 3.0) with robust out-of-sample evidence: the dollar factor (PC1), carry, and momentum. PPP-based value is marginal (t=1.5-2.5). Exotic factors (output gap, external balance, Taylor rule residual) are subsumed by carry + momentum in spanning regressions. Multi-factor FX products based on exotic factors are selling noise.
evidence: PPP value at t-stat 1.5-2.5 falls short of HLZ threshold
Exotic factors subsumed by carry + momentum in spanning regressions
Not directly contested in any debate
open_questions: Whether PPP value factor's marginal significance reflects genuine long-run information or in-sample overfitting
Whether new factors will emerge from alternative data sources that survive HLZ thresholds
